verses.text: His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; the Pharisees object. Petty fault-finding. They watch to accuse rather than to learn.
His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; the Pharisees object. Petty fault-finding. They watch to accuse rather than to learn.
David ate the shewbread; the Son of man is 'Lord also of the sabbath.' For our good. The day's Lord allows mercy and need.
David ate the shewbread; the Son of man is 'Lord also of the sabbath.' For our good. The day's Lord allows mercy and need.
David ate the shewbread; the Son of man is 'Lord also of the sabbath.' For our good. The day's Lord allows mercy and need.
Knowing their thoughts, He bids a man with a withered hand stand forth. Open challenge. He will not hide from doing good on the sabbath.
Knowing their thoughts, He bids a man with a withered hand stand forth. Open challenge. He will not hide from doing good on the sabbath.
Knowing their thoughts, He bids a man with a withered hand stand forth. Open challenge. He will not hide from doing good on the sabbath.
Is it lawful to do good or evil? He heals the hand; they are filled with madness. Goodness enrages. His mercy only deepens their hatred.
Is it lawful to do good or evil? He heals the hand; they are filled with madness. Goodness enrages. His mercy only deepens their hatred.
Is it lawful to do good or evil? He heals the hand; they are filled with madness. Goodness enrages. His mercy only deepens their hatred.
He prays all night on a mountain, then chooses twelve apostles. Prayerful choice. Great decisions are bathed in prayer.
He prays all night on a mountain, then chooses twelve apostles. Prayerful choice. Great decisions are bathed in prayer.
He names the twelve, ending with 'Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.' A solemn list. Even the chosen band held one who fell.
He names the twelve, ending with 'Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.' A solemn list. Even the chosen band held one who fell.
He names the twelve, ending with 'Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.' A solemn list. Even the chosen band held one who fell.
On a plain the multitude seek to touch Him, for 'virtue... healed them all.' Healing power. Mercy flows from Him to every needy soul.
On a plain the multitude seek to touch Him, for 'virtue... healed them all.' Healing power. Mercy flows from Him to every needy soul.
On a plain the multitude seek to touch Him, for 'virtue... healed them all.' Healing power. Mercy flows from Him to every needy soul.
Blessed are ye poor, hungry, weeping, and hated for His sake; great is your reward. Surprising blessing. God's favour rests on the afflicted who are His.
Blessed are ye poor, hungry, weeping, and hated for His sake; great is your reward. Surprising blessing. God's favour rests on the afflicted who are His.
Blessed are ye poor, hungry, weeping, and hated for His sake; great is your reward. Surprising blessing. God's favour rests on the afflicted who are His.
Blessed are ye poor, hungry, weeping, and hated for His sake; great is your reward. Surprising blessing. God's favour rests on the afflicted who are His.
But woe to you rich, full, laughing, and praised; ye have your consolation. Hollow comfort. The world's ease is a poor and passing reward.
But woe to you rich, full, laughing, and praised; ye have your consolation. Hollow comfort. The world's ease is a poor and passing reward.
But woe to you rich, full, laughing, and praised; ye have your consolation. Hollow comfort. The world's ease is a poor and passing reward.
Love your enemies, do good to haters; 'as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also.' The golden rule. Love returns good for evil, as we wish to be treated.
Love your enemies, do good to haters; 'as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also.' The golden rule. Love returns good for evil, as we wish to be treated.
Love your enemies, do good to haters; 'as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also.' The golden rule. Love returns good for evil, as we wish to be treated.
Love your enemies, do good to haters; 'as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also.' The golden rule. Love returns good for evil, as we wish to be treated.
Love your enemies, do good to haters; 'as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also.' The golden rule. Love returns good for evil, as we wish to be treated.
To love only friends is no more than sinners do. No credit. Christ calls to a love that goes beyond the natural.
To love only friends is no more than sinners do. No credit. Christ calls to a love that goes beyond the natural.
To love only friends is no more than sinners do. No credit. Christ calls to a love that goes beyond the natural.
Love your enemies, and be His children; 'Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father... is merciful.' God-like mercy. We resemble God when we show undeserved kindness.
Love your enemies, and be His children; 'Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father... is merciful.' God-like mercy. We resemble God when we show undeserved kindness.
Judge not; forgive and be forgiven; give, and 'good measure... shall men give into your bosom.' Generous return. God repays the merciful with overflowing measure.
Judge not; forgive and be forgiven; give, and 'good measure... shall men give into your bosom.' Generous return. God repays the merciful with overflowing measure.
Can the blind lead the blind? cast out thine own beam before thy brother's mote. Self first. Correct yourself before you correct another.
Can the blind lead the blind? cast out thine own beam before thy brother's mote. Self first. Correct yourself before you correct another.
Can the blind lead the blind? cast out thine own beam before thy brother's mote. Self first. Correct yourself before you correct another.
Can the blind lead the blind? cast out thine own beam before thy brother's mote. Self first. Correct yourself before you correct another.
Every tree is known by its fruit; 'of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.' Fruit and speech. The life and the words reveal the heart.
Every tree is known by its fruit; 'of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.' Fruit and speech. The life and the words reveal the heart.
Every tree is known by its fruit; 'of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.' Fruit and speech. The life and the words reveal the heart.
Why call Me Lord and do not what I say? he that hears and does builds on rock; the careless on sand. Doing, not hearing. Only obeyed truth gives a sure foundation.
